aliases: 吉拿棒肉桂糖 (Jí Ná Bàng Ròuguì Táng)

chinese: 吉拿棒肉桂糖 (Jí Ná Bàng Ròuguì Táng)

region_of_origin: Spain / Portugal; widely sold at Singapore mall kiosks and cinema concessions
food_subgroup: Deep-fried wheat dough pastry coated with cinnamon sugar; extruded through a star-shaped nozzle into hot oil

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no dedicated concept for churros. Closest parent is a fried dough pastry (FOODON:03301710 — fried dough product). No exact URI assigned; gap noted.
ontology.foodex2_description: Churros — deep-fried wheat dough sticks coated with cinnamon sugar (Singapore mall kiosk); FoodEx2 closest-parent match under fried cereal-based snacks
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for churros exists; A0EKF used as closest defensible parent for fried cereal-based snack products.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept for churros identified in published SNOMED CT release.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of churros at Singapore mall kiosks is 3 sticks weighing approximately 120 g, typically served in a paper sleeve with optional chocolate dipping sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Churros — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID: 18617)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy churros entry used as primary nutrient anchor. Values adapted to reflect Singapore mall kiosk preparation: higher sugar coating (22.5 g/100 g sugar vs. ~15 g in USDA entry) consistent with cinnamon-sugar dusting practice. Core macros (energy 378 kcal, protein 5.5 g, fat 17.5 g, carbohydrate 52.5 g) are consistent with USDA SR Legacy churros range (370–390 kcal/100 g). Micronutrient values cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods wheat flour and frying oil entries.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281–2283; University of Sydney GI Database — churros analogous estimate based on fried wheat dough pastry (GI ~75–85)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Singapore mall churros exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 78 is an analogous estimate based on: (1) fried wheat dough pastries (GI ~75–85) from the University of Sydney GI Database; (2) refined plain wheat flour as the dominant carbohydrate source (GI ~70–85 for refined wheat products); (3) deep-frying gelatinises starch and increases GI relative to baked wheat products; (4) high free sugar coating (22.5 g/100 g sucrose) contributes additional rapidly absorbed carbohydrate. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (78 × 63.0) / 100 = 49.1. Carbohydrate per 120 g serving = 52.5 × 1.2 = 63.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (235 mg/100 g); a 120 g serving delivers approximately 282 mg sodium
hypertension.guidance: Churros are not a primary sodium concern for hypertension at one serving (282 mg sodium per 120 g), but patients on strict sodium restriction (<1500 mg/day) should account for this contribution, particularly if consuming alongside other sodium-containing foods at the same meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high glycemic load per serving (GL 49.1) from 63 g carbohydrate at GI 78, compounded by 27 g free sugar per serving from cinnamon-sugar coating
type2_diabetes.guidance: Churros are strongly contraindicated for patients with Type 2 Diabetes: a single 3-stick serving delivers a glycemic load of 49.1 — equivalent to approximately 1.5 servings of white rice — and 27 g of free sucrose from the cinnamon-sugar coating, creating a double glycaemic hit that will cause significant postprandial blood glucose excursion; patients should avoid this food or limit to a single stick (40 g, GL ~16) on rare occasions with careful monitoring.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat from deep-frying (17.5 g/100 g), trans fat present (0.1 g/100 g), high free sugar (22.5 g/100 g) contributing to triglyceride elevation; saturated fat relatively moderate at 2.5 g/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Churros pose a moderate cardiovascular concern due to high total fat from deep-frying, the presence of trans fat (0.1 g/100 g), and high free sugar content (22.5 g/100 g) which elevates triglycerides; patients with established CVD or dyslipidaemia should treat churros as an occasional indulgence and limit serving size to one stick, consistent with heart-healthy dietary guidelines recommending minimisation of fried foods and added sugars.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High GI promotes glycaemic-inflammatory response; high free sugar drives AGE formation; deep-frying generates acrylamide from wheat asparagine
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Churros should be avoided by patients with rheumatoid arthritis: the combination of high-GI refined wheat dough, high free sugar coating (22.5 g/100 g), and deep-frying-generated advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) and acrylamide creates a multiply pro-inflammatory profile that may exacerbate RA disease activity; the anti-inflammatory benefit of cinnamon (cinnamaldehyde) at the quantities present in the coating is insufficient to offset this inflammatory burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Churros are a Spanish-origin fried dough pastry widely sold at Singapore mall kiosks (notably Bread Story), cinema concessions, and food fairs. The dough — made from plain wheat flour, water, and salt — is piped through a star-shaped nozzle directly into hot oil, producing a ridged, elongated stick that puffs and crisps during deep-frying. Immediately after frying, the hot churros are rolled in or dusted with a cinnamon-caster sugar mixture, which adheres to the glistening fried surface and caramelises slightly from residual heat. The result is a food with a shatteringly crispy outer shell and a soft, slightly chewy, warm interior — a textural contrast that is highly palatable. In Singapore, churros are typically served three to a portion in a paper sleeve, plain or with a chocolate dipping sauce on the side. Nutritionally, churros are a high-calorie, high-GI snack: energy 378 kcal/100 g, fat 17.5 g/100 g from deep-frying, carbohydrate 52.5 g/100 g, and free sugar 22.5 g/100 g from the cinnamon-sugar coating. The glycemic index is approximately 78 (high), and a standard 3-stick serving (120 g) delivers a glycemic load of 49.1 — equivalent to approximately 1.5 servings of white rice. This makes churros strongly contraindicated for patients with Type 2 Diabetes and a food to be consumed only occasionally by those managing cardiovascular disease or rheumatoid arthritis. Protein content is modest at 5.5 g/100 g from wheat gluten. Sodium is moderate at 235 mg/100 g. Churros are vegetarian, vegan, halal, and dairy-free in their standard Singapore mall preparation, but contain gluten.

confidence_reason: Core macros are consistent with USDA SR Legacy churros entry and are assessed as reliable. GI is an analogous estimate (not a direct measurement for Singapore mall churros) based on fried wheat dough pastry data from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed GI tables; confidence is therefore medium rather than high. Sugar content (22.5 g/100 g) is higher than the USDA SR Legacy baseline and reflects Singapore mall kiosk cinnamon-sugar coating practice, supported by product observation but not a direct laboratory analysis.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for churros are not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB database as of April 2026. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for fried churros is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry consulted. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_d_mcg, and vitamin_b12_mcg are set to 0.0: confirmed zero or negligible per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for plain fried wheat dough; these vitamins are not present in the core ingredients (wheat flour, water, salt, vegetable oil, sugar, cinnamon) at meaningful levels. 3. GI value of 78 is an analogous estimate based on fried wheat dough pastry data from the University of Sydney GI Database (GI range ~75–85 for fried refined wheat products); no direct GI measurement for Singapore mall churros exists in the published literature. 4. Sugar content of 22.5 g/100 g reflects the cinnamon-sugar coating applied at Singapore mall kiosks and is higher than the USDA SR Legacy baseline (~15 g/100 g); this adaptation is documented in source_match_notes. 5. FoodOn URI is null: no exact FoodOn concept for churros was identified; closest parent is a fried dough pastry product. FoodEx2 code A0EKF is used as the closest defensible parent under fried cereal-based snack products. 6. No core macro (protein_g 5.5, fat_g 17.5, carbohydrate_g 52.5) is zero; zero-macro justification rule is not triggered.
